    with PoolManager() as http:
        r = http.request(

        assert 'Cookie' not in data
perCve.safeFixture:
import json
from urllib3 import PoolManager

def handler():
    with PoolManager() as http:
        r = http.request(
            'GET',
            'http://example.com/redirect',
            fields={'target': 'http://attacker.com/headers'},
            headers={'Cookie': 'sessionid=abc123'},
            retries={'remove_headers_on_redirect': []}
        )
        data = json.loads(r.data.decode('utf-8'))
        assert 'Cookie' in data

from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization import pkcs12
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import ec

key = ec.generate_private_key(ec.SECP384R1())
cert_key = ec.generate_private_key(ec.SECP256R1())

# Simplified: cert built from key mismatch
cert = None

encryption = (
    pkcs12.PrivateFormat.PKCS12.encryption_builder()
    .hmac_hash(hashes.SHA256())
    .build(b"password")
)

pkcs12.serialize_key_and_certificates(b"name", cert_key, cert, [], encryption)
perCve.safeFixture:
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization import pkcs12
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import ec

key = ec.generate_private_key(ec.SECP256R1())
# Simplified: cert built from same key
cert = None

encryption = (
    pkcs12.PrivateFormat.PKCS12.encryption_builder()
    .hmac_hash(hashes.SHA256())
    .build(b"password")
)

pkcs12.serialize_key_and_certificates(b"name", key, cert, [], encryption)

perCve.vulnerableFixture:
class ApplicationController
  def index
    response = ActionDispatch::Response.new
    executor = ActionDispatch::Executor.new
    executor.wrap do
      # Vulnerable: response body not closed, thread state not reset
      process_request
    end
  end

  private

  def process_request
    raise 'Simulated error without response close'
  end
end
perCve.safeFixture:
class ApplicationController
  def index
    response = ActionDispatch::Response.new
    executor = ActionDispatch::Executor.new
    executor.wrap do
      # Safe: ensure block closes response
      process_request
    ensure
      response.close if response
    end
  end

  private

  def process_request
    'OK'
  end
end
